an9.15:1.1: “Monks, it’s just as if there were a boil that had been building for many years
an9.15:1.2: with nine openings, nine un-lanced heads.
an9.15:1.3: Whatever would ooze out from it
an9.15:1.4: would be an uncleanliness oozing out, a stench oozing out, a disgust oozing out.
an9.15:1.5: Whatever would be discharged from it
an9.15:1.6: would be an uncleanliness discharging, a stench discharging, a disgust discharging.
an9.15:2.1: “’A boil,’ monks, is another word for this body composed of the four properties, born of mother & father, fed on rice & porridge, subject to inconstancy, rubbing & abrasion, breaking-up & disintegrating.
an9.15:2.2: It has nine openings, nine un-lanced heads.
an9.15:2.3: Whatever would ooze out from it
an9.15:2.4: would be an uncleanliness oozing out, a stench oozing out, a disgust oozing out.
an9.15:2.5: Whatever would be discharged from it
an9.15:2.6: would be an uncleanliness discharging, a stench discharging, a disgust discharging.
an9.15:2.7: For that reason, you should become disenchanted with this body.”
